Hi Emmanuele, thanks for the numbers. How does Brazil compare to last year?
Hi David. Brazil is much stronger than last year. Our growth is higher, and access is faster.
Great. And which indication is performing the best?
Atopic dermatitis is our best indication. It is bigger than asthma in the private market.
And how do you compare to your main competitor?
We have one clear advantage: higher efficacy. That is why we are ahead of the curve.
Impressive. Is this your most important market in the region?
Yes. Brazil is one of the most important markets in our portfolio, and we are outperforming the forecast.
Brazil is one of the most important markets for us.
We are ahead of the curve on efficacy.

Good morning, everyone. Let me give you a quick comparison of the regions this quarter. Brazil is our strongest market right now. Growth is higher than last year, and access is much better in the private sector. In fact, Brazil is outperforming the forecast for the third quarter in a row. Compared to last year, our biggest indication, atopic dermatitis, is growing faster than asthma. Our main competitor is still ahead in coverage, but our efficacy data gives us a clear advantage. Overall, Brazil is one of the most important markets in the portfolio, and the numbers are the best we have seen. Great work, team.

Hi Emmanuele, it's Claire from the Global team. Quick note before tomorrow's review. The VP loves comparisons, so please be ready to compare. Three things. First, tell him how Brazil compares to last year. Is it stronger? Is growth higher? Second, tell him which indication is your best, and why. And third, be ready to compare Brazil to the main competitor. Where is our advantage? Keep it simple. Bigger, better, higher, faster. He wants the big picture, not every number. You have the best story in the region, so tell it with confidence. Talk soon.

Hi David, let me compare the numbers for you. Compared to last year, Brazil is much stronger. Our growth is higher, and access is faster. Atopic dermatitis is our best indication. And we are outperforming the forecast this quarter.
